sourceQuestion:
In how many ways can 2 distinct scholarships be awarded to 4 applicants, when each scholarship is given to a different applicant?

A. 4  B. 12  C. 64  D. 51

parsed.stem: In how many ways can 2 distinct scholarships be awarded to 4 applicants, when each scholarship is given to a different applicant?

sourceQuestion:
Ten teams participate in a tournament. Every team plays each of the other teams twice. The total number of matches to be played is:

A. 20  B. 45  C. 60  D. 90

parsed.stem: Ten teams participate in a tournament. Every team plays each of the other teams twice. The total number of matches to be played is:

sourceQuestion:
A five digit number is formed using the digits 1, 3, 5, 7, 9 without repeating any of them. What is the sum of all such possible five digit numbers?

A. 6666600  B. 6666600  C. 0000000  D. 0000000

parsed.stem: A five digit number is formed using the digits 1, 3, 5, 7, 9 without repeating any of them. What is the sum of all such possible five digit numbers?

sourceQuestion:
Five teams have to compete in a league, with every team playing every other team exactly once, before going to the next round. How many matches will have to be held to complete the league round of matches?

A. 20  B. 10  C. 8  D. 5

parsed.stem: Five teams have to compete in a league, with every team playing every other team exactly once, before going to the next round. How many matches will have to be held to complete the league round of matches?

sourceQuestion:
The number of 3-digit numbers such that the digit 1 is never to the immediate right of 2 is
A. 781
B. 791
C. 881
D. 891

parsed.stem: The number of 3-digit numbers such that the digit 1 is never to the immediate right of 2 is

sourceQuestion:
There are 4 women W_1, W_2, W_3, W_4 and 5 men M_1, M_2, M_3, M_4, M_5 in a group. We are required to form 4 pairs each consisting of one woman and one man. W_1 is not to be paired with M_1, and M_4 must necessarily be paired with someone. In how many ways can such 4 pairs be formed?
A. 74
B. 76
C. 78
D. 80

parsed.stem: There are 4 women W_1, W_2, W_3, W_4 and 5 men M_1, M_2, M_3, M_4, M_5 in a group. We are required to form 4 pairs each consisting of one woman and one man. W_1 is not to be paired with M_1, and M_4 must necessarily be paired with someone. In how many ways can such 4 pairs be formed?

sourceQuestion:
A three-member committee has to be formed from a group of 9 people. How many such distinct committees can be formed?
A. 27
B. 72
C. 81
D. 84

parsed.stem: A three-member committee has to be formed from a group of 9 people. How many such distinct committees can be formed?

sourceQuestion:
Five different books (P, Q, R, S, T) are to be arranged on a shelf. The books R and S are to be arranged first and second, respectively from the right side of the shelf. The number of different orders in which P, Q and T may be arranged is ______.
A. 2
B. 6
C. 12
D. 120

parsed.stem: Five different books (P, Q, R, S, T) are to be arranged on a shelf. The books R and S are to be arranged first and second, respectively from the right side of the shelf. The number of different orders in which P, Q and T may be arranged is ______.

sourceQuestion:
Person 1 and Person 2 invest in three mutual funds A, B and C. The amounts they invest in each of these mutual funds are given in the table.

| | Mutual fund A | Mutual fund B | Mutual fund C |
|---|---|---|---|
| Person 1 | 10,000 | 20,000 | 15,000 |
| Person 2 | 20,000 | 10,000 | 15,000 |

At the end of one year, the total amount that Person 1 gets is Rs. 500 more than Person 2. The annual rate of return for the mutual funds B and C is 15% each. What is the annual rate of return for mutual fund A?
A. 7.5%
B. 10%
C. 15%
D. 20%

parsed.stem:
Person 1 and Person 2 invest in three mutual funds A, B and C. The amounts they invest in each of these mutual funds are given in the table.

| | Mutual fund A | Mutual fund B | Mutual fund C |
|---|---|---|---|
| Person 1 | 10,000 | 20,000 | 15,000 |
| Person 2 | 20,000 | 10,000 | 15,000 |

At the end of one year, the total amount that Person 1 gets is Rs. 500 more than Person 2. The annual rate of return for the mutual funds B and C is 15% each. What is the annual rate of return for mutual fund A?

sourceQuestion:
The current population of a city is 11,02,500. If it has been increasing at the rate of 5% per annum, what was its population 2 years ago?

A. 9,32,500  B. 9,95,006  C. 10,00,000  D. 12,51,506

parsed.stem: The current population of a city is 11,02,500. If it has been increasing at the rate of 5% per annum, what was its population 2 years ago?

sourceQuestion:
A person invests Rs. 1000 at 10% annual compound interest for 2 years. At the end of two years, the whole amount is invested at an annual simple interest of 12% for 5 years. The total value of the investment finally is:

A. 1776  B. 1780  C. 1920  D. 1196

parsed.stem: A person invests Rs. 1000 at 10% annual compound interest for 2 years. At the end of two years, the whole amount is invested at an annual simple interest of 12% for 5 years. The total value of the investment finally is:

sourceQuestion:
The population of a new city is 5 million and is growing at 20% annually. How many years would it take to double at this growth rate?

A. 3 - 4 years  B. 4 - 5 years  C. 5 - 6 years  D. 6 - 7 years

parsed.stem: The population of a new city is 5 million and is growing at 20% annually. How many years would it take to double at this growth rate?

sourceQuestion:
Leila aspires to buy a car worth Rs. 6,67,000 after 5 years. What is the minimum amount in Rupees that she should deposit now in a bank which offers 10% annual rate of interest, if the interest was compounded annually?

A. 5,00,000  B. 4,14,333  C. 4,00,000  D. 7,50,000

parsed.stem: Leila aspires to buy a car worth Rs. 6,67,000 after 5 years. What is the minimum amount in Rupees that she should deposit now in a bank which offers 10% annual rate of interest, if the interest was compounded annually?

sourceQuestion:
An automobile plant contracted to buy shock absorbers from two suppliers X and Y. X supplies 60% of the shock absorbers and Y supplies 40% of the shock absorbers. All shock absorbers are subjected to a quality test. The ones that pass the quality test are considered reliable. Of X's shock absorbers, 96% are reliable. Of Y's shock absorbers, 72% are reliable. The probability that a randomly chosen shock absorber, which is found to be reliable, is made by Y is:

A. 0.288  B. 0.334  C. 0.667  D. 0.720

parsed.stem: An automobile plant contracted to buy shock absorbers from two suppliers X and Y. X supplies 60% of the shock absorbers and Y supplies 40% of the shock absorbers. All shock absorbers are subjected to a quality test. The ones that pass the quality test are considered reliable. Of X's shock absorbers, 96% are reliable. Of Y's shock absorbers, 72% are reliable. The probability that a randomly chosen shock absorber, which is found to be reliable, is made by Y is:

sourceQuestion:
A box contains 15 blue balls and 6 black balls. If 2 balls are selected randomly, without replacement, the probability of an outcome in which the first selected is a blue ball and the second selected is a black ball is:

A. 1/7  B. 90/462  C. 1/2  D. 5/11

parsed.stem: A box contains 15 blue balls and 6 black balls. If 2 balls are selected randomly, without replacement, the probability of an outcome in which the first selected is a blue ball and the second selected is a black ball is:

sourceQuestion:
In a factory, two machines M1 and M2 manufacture 70% and 30% of the autocomponents respectively. Out of the total production, 1% of M1 and 10% of M2 are found to be defective. If a randomly drawn autocomponent from the combined lot is found defective, what is the probability that it was manufactured by M2?

A. 3/14  B. 7/13  C. 10/13  D. 14/13

parsed.stem: In a factory, two machines M1 and M2 manufacture 70% and 30% of the autocomponents respectively. Out of the total production, 1% of M1 and 10% of M2 are found to be defective. If a randomly drawn autocomponent from the combined lot is found defective, what is the probability that it was manufactured by M2?

sourceQuestion:
You are given three coins: one has heads on both faces, the second has tails on both faces, and the third has a head on one face and a tail on the other. You choose a coin at random and toss it, and it comes up heads. The probability that the other face is tails is

A. 1/4  B. 1/3  C. 1/2  D. 2/3

parsed.stem: You are given three coins: one has heads on both faces, the second has tails on both faces, and the third has a head on one face and a tail on the other. You choose a coin at random and toss it, and it comes up heads. The probability that the other face is tails is

sourceQuestion:
A coin is tossed thrice. Let X be the event that head occurs in each of the first two tosses. Let Y be the event that a tail occurs on the third toss. Let Z be the event that two tails occur in three tosses. Based on the above information, which one of the following statements is TRUE?

A. X and Y are not independent
B. Y and Z are not independent
C. X and Z are dependent
D. Y and Z are independent
sourceAnswer: B. Y and Z are not independent

parsed.stem: A coin is tossed thrice. Let X be the event that head occurs in each of the first two tosses. Let Y be the event that a tail occurs on the third toss. Let Z be the event that two tails occur in three tosses. Based on the above information, which one of the following statements is TRUE?

options.text: X and Y are not independent
Y and Z are not independent

sourceQuestion:
Consider a square sheet of side 1 unit. In the first step, it is cut along the main diagonal to get two triangles. In the next step, one of the cut triangles is revolved about its short edge to form a solid cone. The volume of the resulting cone, in cubic units, is

A. pi/3  B. 2pi/3  C. pi  D. 3pi

parsed.stem: Consider a square sheet of side 1 unit. In the first step, it is cut along the main diagonal to get two triangles. In the next step, one of the cut triangles is revolved about its short edge to form a solid cone. The volume of the resulting cone, in cubic units, is

sourceQuestion:
A contour line joins locations having the same height above the mean sea level. The following is a contour plot of a geographical region. Contour lines are shown at 25-m intervals in this plot. If in a flood, the water level rises to 525 m, which of the following villages P, Q, R, S, T get submerged?

A. P, Q  B. P, Q, T  C. R, S, T  D. Q, R, S

parsed.stem: A contour line joins locations having the same height above the mean sea level. The following is a contour plot of a geographical region. Contour lines are shown at 25-m intervals in this plot. If in a flood, the water level rises to 525 m, which of the following villages P, Q, R, S, T get submerged?

sourceQuestion:
An air pressure contour line joins locations in a region having the same atmospheric pressure. The following is an air pressure contour plot of a geographical region. Contour lines are shown at 0.05 bar intervals in this plot. If the possibility of a thunderstorm is given by how fast air pressure rises or drops over a region, which of the following regions is most likely to have a thunderstorm?

A. P
B. Q
C. R
D. S

parsed.stem: An air pressure contour line joins locations in a region having the same atmospheric pressure. The following is an air pressure contour plot of a geographical region. Contour lines are shown at 0.05 bar intervals in this plot. If the possibility of a thunderstorm is given by how fast air pressure rises or drops over a region, which of the following regions is most likely to have a thunderstorm?

sourceQuestion:
A shopkeeper buys shirts from a producer and sells them at 20% profit. A customer has to pay Rs.1,770.00 including 18% GST, per shirt. At what price did the shopkeeper buy each shirt from the producer?

A. Rs.800  B. Rs.1,975.40  C. Rs.1,250.00  D. Rs.2,548.80

parsed.stem: A shopkeeper buys shirts from a producer and sells them at 20% profit. A customer has to pay Rs.1,770.00 including 18% GST, per shirt. At what price did the shopkeeper buy each shirt from the producer?

sourceQuestion:
The variable cost (V) of manufacturing a product varies according to the equation V = 4q where q is the quantity produced. The fixed cost (F) of production of the same product reduces with q according to the equation F = 400/q. How many units should be produced to minimize the total cost (V + F)?

A. 5  B. 4  C. 7  D. 6

parsed.stem: The variable cost (V) of manufacturing a product varies according to the equation V = 4q where q is the quantity produced. The fixed cost (F) of production of the same product reduces with q according to the equation F = 400/q. How many units should be produced to minimize the total cost (V + F)?

sourceQuestion:
The cost function for a product in a firm is given by 5q^2, where q is the amount of production. The firm can sell the product at a market price of 50 per unit. The number of units to be produced by the firm such that the profit is maximized is:

A. 5  B. 10  C. 15  D. 25

parsed.stem: The cost function for a product in a firm is given by 5q^2, where q is the amount of production. The firm can sell the product at a market price of 50 per unit. The number of units to be produced by the firm such that the profit is maximized is:

sourceQuestion:
Ten friends planned to share equally the cost of buying a gift for their teacher. When two of them decided not to contribute, each of the other friends had to pay Rs.150 more. The cost of the gift was Rs. ____

A. 600  B. 6000  C. 1200  D. 12000

parsed.stem: Ten friends planned to share equally the cost of buying a gift for their teacher. When two of them decided not to contribute, each of the other friends had to pay Rs.150 more. The cost of the gift was Rs. ____

sourceQuestion:
The price of an item is 10% cheaper in an online store S compared to the price at another online store M. Store S charges Rs.150 for delivery. There are no delivery charges for orders from store M. A person bought the items from store S and saved Rs.75. What is the price of the item at the online store M (in Rs.), if there are no other charges than those described above?

A. 2500  B. 2250  C. 1750  D. 1500

parsed.stem: The price of an item is 10% cheaper in an online store S compared to the price at another online store M. Store S charges Rs.150 for delivery. There are no delivery charges for orders from store M. A person bought the items from store S and saved Rs.75. What is the price of the item at the online store M (in Rs.), if there are no other charges than those described above?

sourceQuestion:
There are 4 red, N green, and 6 blue balls inside a box. If N number of balls are picked simultaneously, what is the smallest value of N that guarantees there will be at least two balls of the same colour? One cannot see the colour of the balls until they are picked.

A. 4
B. 15
C. 5
D. 2

parsed.stem: There are 4 red, N green, and 6 blue balls inside a box. If N number of balls are picked simultaneously, what is the smallest value of N that guarantees there will be at least two balls of the same colour? One cannot see the colour of the balls until they are picked.

sourceQuestion:
If P, Q, R, S are four individuals, how many teams of size exceeding one can be formed, with Q as a member?

A. 5
B. 6
C. 7
D. 8

parsed.stem: If P, Q, R, S are four individuals, how many teams of size exceeding one can be formed, with Q as a member?

sourceQuestion:
How many pairs of sets (S, T) are possible among the subsets of {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6} that satisfy the condition that T is a subset of S?

A. 729
B. 728
C. 60
D. 665

parsed.stem: How many pairs of sets (S, T) are possible among the subsets of {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6} that satisfy the condition that T is a subset of S?

sourceQuestion:
There are 5 Indians and 3 Chinese in a group of 8 people. How many subgroups of this group can we choose so that every subgroup has at least one Indian?

A. 56
B. 52
C. 81
D. 44

parsed.stem: There are 5 Indians and 3 Chinese in a group of 8 people. How many subgroups of this group can we choose so that every subgroup has at least one Indian?
